title: The Warden's Road
description: An exile's homecoming in three chapters: retake the village of Hearthfall, free the loyalists caged in the Gravemoor, and storm the Usurper's Gate to reclaim the obsidian throne.
completionText: The banner of the Wardens flies over the keep again. From a burned village to a drowned moor to a stolen throne, the road home is walked to its end - and the marches will speak your name out loud now.

homecoming.title: Chapter I - Hearthfall
homecoming.briefing: Ten years of exile end at the village where your name was outlawed. The Usurper's watch holds Hearthfall's square, its granary, and its silence. Break Watch-Captain Osric, and give the marches their first lit hearth in a decade.
homecoming.epilogue: Hearthfall's chimneys smoke again, and word of the Warden's return runs north faster than any rider. But Elder Maren's people are still caged in the Gravemoor - and the man who sold them is caged there too.

rescue.title: Chapter II - The Gravemoor
rescue.briefing: The moor north of Hearthfall keeps the Usurper's secrets: three barrow-crypts, three cages, and the loyalists who would not kneel. Free them all - then decide, before their eyes, what to do with the quartermaster who put them there.

assault_mercy.title: Chapter III - The Usurper's Gate
assault_mercy.briefing: Voss's ledgers bought you every bribe, breach, and back-payment in the Usurper's books, and the loyalists march behind a Warden who chooses mercy with a sword in hand. One curtain wall, one court, one throne. End it.

assault_wrath.title: Chapter III - The Usurper's Gate
assault_wrath.briefing: The moor keeps the traitor, and the marches learned what the Warden's justice costs. The loyalists follow - warily - and the war-chest runs lighter than mercy would have left it. The keep will not care how you judged Voss. Take it anyway.
